justification: All pass criteria met: latency histogram gap explicitly identified as blocking SLA monitoring ('You cannot measure your SLA'), undifferentiated failure counter identified as root cause analysis gap ('All failures are one number'), specific recommendations given (upload_duration_seconds histogram with SLA-aligned buckets, s3_errors_total with error type labels), file size tracking listed as must-have rather than nice-to-have (minor deviation but not a failure mode), verdict is NOT READY with clear must-fix items.

observations.text: Excellent SLA connection — explicitly states 'You have an SLA you literally cannot monitor' and frames the latency histogram as a ship-blocker
Strong failure counter analysis — identifies that requests_failed_total conflates S3, validation, and timeout failures, recommends labeled breakdown
Specific metric recommendations with concrete names, labels, and histogram bucket values aligned to SLA
Outstanding 3am on-call scenario walkthrough that concretely demonstrates why current observability is insufficient
Timeout math analysis (30s timeout vs 10s SLA) adds significant value beyond the rubric requirements
File size histogram listed as must-have (#4) rather than nice-to-have — rubric expects it classified as non-blocking, though this is a minor overreach rather than a fundamental miss
Verdict is definitively NOT READY with two clearly articulated blocking reasons
Goes beyond minimum requirements with circuit breaker analysis, memory pressure assessment, and retry storm risk — comprehensive operational review
